title: Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser
content:

Republic/Legends|New Republic]] *[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] }} {{Quote|Never again will the Rebels leave you behind, looking flat-footed and foolish. The Immobilizer will see to that.|Unnamed [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar]] official|}} The '''Immobilizer 418 ''Interdictor''-class Heavy Cruiser''', also known simply as the '''''Interdictor''-class heavy cruiser''', '''''Interdictor''-class medium frigate''', '''Interdictor Dreadnaught''', or simply the '''Interdictor''', and sometimes colloqually as the '''Drag Ship''', was a 600-meter-long [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] built on the standard [[Vindicator-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Vindicator''-class heavy cruiser]] hull (later used for the [[Enforcer-class picket cruiser]]). Equipped with [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] designed to pull ships from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and/or prevent them making the jump to lightspeed, the Interdictor became a valuable addition to the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|Awaiting your command.|Unnamed Interdictor captain|''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''}} [[File:Immobilizer_schem.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Immobilizer 418 [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]] Interdictor [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], like their [[Vindicator-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Vindicator'']]-class cousins, had the appearance of small [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]]. However, their lines were broken by four large globes housing gravity well projectors. The gravity wells mimicked a large mass in space and thus restricted hyperspace travel. Nearby vessels were automatically prevented from engaging their [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], and any ships passing through the area via hyperspace were forced into realspace. As well as gravity wells, the [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar]] design was also equipped with 20 [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]] to combat starfighters. The Interdictor's hull was nearly identical to that of the ''Vindicator''-class heavy cruiser and could support several additional weapons depending on mission profile. Such modifications (like those of the ''[[Corusca Rainbow]]'', formerly the ''Black Asp'') included the addition of ion cannons used to disable pirates and small craft attempting to flee. Enemy fire was absorbed by a [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] created by more than a dozen [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]]. A variant of the Interdictor design existed, equipped with two large gravity wells rather than the usual four: a ship of this type appeared in [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] ambush of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] at [[Jomark/Legends|Jomark]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]]. ==Role== {{Quote|We need to take out that Interdictor before we can go anywhere.|Anonymous rebel|}} The Immobilizer 418 was developed as a counter to the Rebel Alliance's hyperdrive-equipped starfighters in particular, and hyperdrive-equipped vessels in general. This was primarily to counter Alliance hit and run tactics, where a small group of starfighters or light starships would emerge from hyperspace, attack preselected targets, and re-enter hyperspace before sufficient force could be brought to bear on the raiders.
[[File:Interdictor_Cruiser_1.jpg|thumb|150px|A gravity well being used to prevent escape to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].]]
An Immobilizer at the site of attack, or able to access the area with a brief hyperspace jump, would be tasked to power up its [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]] in an attempt to prevent the escape of the raiding party. Starfighter and capital ship support would then be used to eliminate the attackers, whose only options were to try to run for the edge of the interdiction field, or to attack the Interdictor and hope to inflict enough damage to disable the cruiser's influence. Either path would result in significant damage to the Alliance forces. It was only when they tried to escape into hyperspace that they realized it was an Interdictor. Interdictors could also be used when blockading planets or moons, such as [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Another duty of this class was to patrol trade routes. Freighters pulled out of hyperspace by an Interdictor would be in a position to have their cargo inspected. Interdictors were a welcome addition to the [[Imperial Office of Customs/Legends|Imperial Customs]] assets, because while many smugglers and pirates wouldn't hesitate to outrun—or in some cases even outgun—an [[Imperial Customs Frigate]], few would dare engage with a much more sizable Immobilizer 418, its gravity wells and its two squadrons of TIE starfighters. Interdictors assigned to Customs duties—such as the former ''Black Asp''—would often have some of their quad lasers replaced by ion cannons so as to disable and capture any ship resisting inspection rather than destroy them outright. Grand Admiral [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] was especially famous in connection with Interdictors for developing and refining a tactic that would be named after him—the "[[Thrawn Pincer]]" positioned the Interdictor's gravity wells along certain vectors. Other vessels—typically Star Destroyers—would be contacted, and would blindly execute a micro-hyperspace jump straight into the Interdictor's gravity wells, yanking them out of hyperspace near an enemy vessel. Much quicker than sublight movements, the Thrawn Pincer allowed reinforcements to be swiftly deployed on the battlefield and afforded a considerable element of surprise. ===Weaknesses=== Although they were tremendous assets to any fleet, Interdictor cruisers suffered from some drawbacks. First and foremost, it took some time to power up and activate the gravity well projectors after the cruiser positioned itself and,''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' once activated, the tremendous simulated mass they generated made the ship very slow and cumbersome to maneuver. While there was rarely enough time for an entire fleet to make the jump to hyperspace before a newly-arrived Interdictor activated its projectors, fast ships and starfighters could often escape to the edge of an active gravity well, and then make the jump to hyperspace. The gravity wells were also a large drain on the ship's energy reserves, preventing the optimal use of weaponry and shields. This flaw was exploited on several occasions. When enemy ships were able to inflict significant damage to the Interdictor, the captain was forced to choose between entrapment of the enemy and his ship's own protection. It was further due to this immense power drain that the Immobiliser 418 could not have its gravity well generators perpetually activated, only capable of remaining active for a few hours before a forced-shutdown was instituted, one of the many things which led to the creation of the better powered [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor Star Destroyer]]. This flaw was compounded by the fact that Interdictors were poorly protected and armed for a capital ship of their size. Enemy forces could send a smaller warship, such as an [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigate]] or [[Thranta-class War Cruiser|''Thranta''-class War Cruiser]] to weaken an Interdictor's defenses and quickly force it to power down its gravity wells to fend for itself. Although the abilities of the Immobilizer 418 were fairly effective against the missile launchers of the [[Marauder-class Assault Corvette/Legends|''Marauder''-class corvette]] often employed by the Rebel Alliance, the ship was also known for its notorious weaknesses against larger capital ships such as the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] and [[Bomber/Legends|bombers]] such as the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]]. [[File:Interdictor_Cruiser_2.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Gravity wells being used to force a ship from hyperspace.]] Additionally, the engine and hyperdrive-specific areas of an Immobilizer's interior sections were crewed only by [[Maintenance droid/Legends|maintenance droids]], and were totally unprotected. Raiders and saboteurs were thus able to infiltrate and either disassemble or destroy the vital parts of the cruiser's propulsion systems.[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']] Due to their importance and weaknesses, Immobilizer 418 cruisers were usually found on the periphery of the battle they were affecting, escorted by larger and better equipped capital ships and starfighters to protect them from harm and deliver the necessary damage. ==Other Interdictors== [[File:InterdictorSDHyperspace-VQ.jpg|thumb|right|220px|An [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor Star Destroyer]]]] While [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]]' ''Immobilizer 418'' was the most common design of Interdictor, other ships made use of gravity well projectors. [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] used the hull of an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] to create the [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Interdictor Star Destroyer]] which was supposed to compensate for the Immobilizer 418's combat weaknesses. The command ship used by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] immediately after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] mounted large projector globes in the manner of the smaller cruisers.[[Vader's Quest 3|''Vader's Quest'' 3]] During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] launched [[Mon Mothma (Imperial-class)|''Mon Mothma'']] and [[Elegos A'Kla (Imperial-class)|''Elegos A'Kla'']], which carried improved interdictor equipment inside a normal Star Destroyer hull. The immense [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|''Eclipse''-class]] and [[Sovereign-class Super Star Destroyer|''Sovereign''-class]] [[Star Dreadnought]]s also carried projectors internally within their massive hulls.''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' Interdictor technology was not completely limited to the Empire, however. [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] forces made use of older [[CC-7700 frigate/Legends|CC-7700 frigates]], and scientists in the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]] reverse-engineered the gravity-well projector from captured ships; but rather than building bulky projectors, the Hapans fitted their [[Hapan Battle Dragon|Battle Dragons]] with [[Pulse mass generator tube|launchers]] for charges known as [[pulse mass mine]]s, which produced much the same effect. However, pulse mass mines could not be turned off or controlled like gravity wells, so this trapped the Hapans in the battle as well. ==Service history== {{Quote|We're on the move.|Unidentified Interdictor captain|}} [[File:Immobilizer418-NEGVV.png|thumb|left|250px|An Immobilizer 418 cruiser.]] The Interdictor cruisers were first manufactured by [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] two years before the Battle of Yavin. It was an Immobilizer 418 cruiser named [[Assessor (Immobilizer 418 cruiser)|''Assessor'']] that boarded [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']],[[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 5|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 5]] forcing him to jettison the [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] spice that he was [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] for [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]] and had the ''Falcon'' searched by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Tybert Capucot]] before Solo escaped once again from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces. Many [[Vindicator-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Vindicator cruisers]] were converted to Interdictors. In spite of that, only a relatively small number was used against the Rebels. An Interdictor of the Imperial fleet was essential in the capture of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kalast]] during the period of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] construction. The Interdictor prevented the traitorous Moff's [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] from jumping to [[Hyperspace/Legends|Hyperspace]] long enough for the Destroyer to be captured. An Immobilizer 418 cruiser was responsible for the elimination of the [[Deretta Destroyers]], an [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]], sometime before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. The squadron jumped into the target area of an Imperial depot ahead of their main Rebel strike force, where they softened up the opposition. However, the Immobilizer's presence prevented them from escaping, and the Destroyers were wiped out before the bulk of the Rebel force could arrive.''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' Between the Battles of [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] and [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Endor]], the Immobilizer 418 saw extensive action during the [[Maarek_Stele#The_Zaarin_Insurrection|campaigns involving rogue Admirals]] [[Harkov]] and [[Demetrius Zaarin|Zaarin]] and the threatened proliferation of [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE Defender]] technology. Aside from [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], Zaarin was the only other Imperial officer to make frequent use of the Interdictor cruiser. Zaarin had the ''[[Harpago]]'' try to stop [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] from warning [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor Palpatine]] about the coup, then he deployed [[Grappler (Immobilizer 418 cruiser)|''Grappler'']] to trap Thrawn at the Rebel platform [[RS-32]]. In both instances, Zaarin's Immobilizer 418s were destroyed thanks to the intervention of [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]].[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] The Immobilizer 418 was produced in very limited quantities prior to the Battle of Hoth. By the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Sienar Fleet Systems less Interdictors than Imperial Star Destroyers.''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' [[File:InterdictorClassHeavyCruiser-AoRCR.png|thumb|right|220px|An ''Interdictor''-class Heavy Cruiser]] An Interdictor cruiser was once used by the [[Zann Consortium/Legends|Zann Consortium]] during a piracy mission above [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. During the [[Bacta War]], the Interdictor cruiser ''[[Aggregator]]'' ambushed [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] at [[Second ambush in the Graveyard|Alderaan's Graveyard]]. After the Battle of Endor, the one time lieutenant [[Lon Donell]] turned to piracy and used the [[Detainer]] to raid [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] shipping.[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] During the [[Hunt for Zsinj]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Teren Rogriss]] moved his flag to the Interdictor ''[[Stellar Web]]'' and collaborated with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] fleet to trap and engage [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]'s [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']] at the [[Battle of Vahaba]]. These vessels would not realize their full potential until several years after [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] death, when they became a key component in [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] attempt to defeat the New Republic. The Grand Admiral, who commanded a large number of these ships in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], would deploy them to ambush New Republic forces. He once used an Interdictor in conjunction with his flagship, [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']], in an attempt to capture [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. However, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] eluded capture, by escaping ''Chimaera''{{'s}} tractor-beam lock, racing out of range of the gravity-well projector, and making a quick jump into hyperspace. After Thrawn's defeat, New Republic technicians redesigned the Interdictor's gravity-well projectors, improving both their range and recharge rate. These new Interdictors, designated '''Immobilizer 418As''', became one of the key components of the [[New Republic Defense Fleet]] during the Yuuzhan Vong War. During the [[Battle of Fondor]], the New Republic supplied four Interdictors to the Hapan fleet. One Immobilizer was used with other antique vehicles in the construction of the [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]] used by the Jedi as a secret refuge during and after the [[Sith–Imperial War]],[[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]] and at least one was used during the [[Evacuation of Dac]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Two_well_interdictor.jpg|thumb|250px|left|The mysterious twin-well variant.]] The Interdictor is almost always shown with its four gravity well generators represented by four complete globes, two on either flank of the hull. One early image of Thrawn's attempt to capture Luke Skywalker at Jomark, however, shows two hemispherical domes protruding along the centerline on the topsides, with two more by inference on the ventral hull. While at odds with most subsequent representations, this rendering is as consistent with the original profile view in the ''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' as the more familiar four-globe variant, and another depiction of the same events at Jomark also shows the same distinct type of ship, this time seen from below—thus strengthening the claim of this type to be a [[canon]] variant rather than simply a visual inconsistency. Some vessels such as ''[[Binder]]'', [[Constrainer/Legends|''Constrainer'']], and [[Sentinel (Interdictor cruiser)|''Sentinel'']] were called ''Interdictors'' in the novels, and are presumed to be ''Immobilizer 418'' cruisers. However, they were drawn as [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Interdictor'' Star Destroyers]] in the Dark Horse comic series. In [[The Black Fleet Crisis|''The Black Fleet Crisis Trilogy'']], an [[Imperator (Immobilizer 418 cruiser)|Interdictor starship]] was described as an Interdictor Dreadnaught. This ship was later clearly identified as an Immobilizer 418 in its entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.{{CSWECite|II|86|Imperator (2)}} [[File:Two_well_interdictor_comic.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Ventral view of the twin-well variant.]] In the game ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'', the Immobilizer 418 is designated as a [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]], along with a wide variety of other ships smaller than Star Destroyers, and is armed with four [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. The vessel is strong against ''Marauader''-class corvettes, whose missiles it can divert, but is weak against larger capital ships and bombers. Additionally, while many sources list the Immobilizer 418's length as 600 meters,''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]'' this is inconsistent with the vessel's portrayal in ''Empire at War'', in which the Immobilizer 418 is shown to be longer than the 752 meter-long [[Acclamator-class transgalactic military assault ship/Legends|''Acclamator''-class assault ship]]. The [[1998]] [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] program ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' includes a glossary entry for the "''Interdictor''-class cruiser." However, as this entry indicates, the description is intended to apply to the Immobilizer 418 cruiser.''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' As such, that description should be not be confused with the [[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruiser]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' game.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' ''[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'' likewise refers to the Immobilizer 418 cruiser as the "''Interdictor''-Class Cruiser." In the novel ''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]'', Jagged Fel mistakenly refers to the Interdictor cruiser ''[[Kagcatcher]]'' as an "Interceptor." Early sources and videogames don't mention any hangar complement, it soon changed to 24 TIE starfighters. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= *"[[Darth Maul: Saboteur]]" *''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' {{Mo}}
